creator: sab@vegamuse.org
text:
Ignore that last message, whatever it was it worked fine when I tried it
 again.  First time gave permission denied when fetching the file.
 
 --
 Stephen J. Kiernan    |          FreeBSD -- The Power To Serve
 sab@vegamuse.org      |           (and play Linux games, too)
 kiernasj@connix.com   |      Currently running FreeBSD 5.0-CURRENT
 
 On Thu, 16 Aug 2001, Patrick Li wrote:
 
 > 
 > >Submitter-Id:	current-users
 > >Originator:	Patrick Li
 > >Organization:	N/A
 > >Confidential:	no
 > >Synopsis:	Update port sysutils/wminet
 > >Severity:	non-critical
 > >Priority:	low
 > >Category:	ports
 > >Class:		change-request
 > >Release:	FreeBSD 4.4-PRERELEASE i386
 > >Environment:
 > System: FreeBSD bottlenek 4.4-PRERELEASE FreeBSD 4.4-PRERELEASE #0: Mon Aug 13 19:08:19 EDT 2001 pat@bottlenek:/usr/src/sys/compile/BOTTLENEK i386
 > >Description:
 > + Update to 2.0.3
 > + Use official site to download tarball
 > + Use ${DOCSDIR}
 > + Quiet install of docs
 > + NOPORTDOCS support in pkg-plist
 > + Use unified format for patches
 > + Strips binary program
 > + Sync readme description with pkg-descr
 > + Use xterm over rxvt in installed config
 >   and add note that lpd monitoring not yet
 >   works.
 > >How-To-Repeat:
 > N/A
 > >Fix:
